figures.caption: Table 1. C-index comparison across five TCGA datasets with (•) and without (-) modalities. P and G denote pathological and genomic modalities, respectively. † indicates it requires identical modalities for training and inference. The bests are in bold and the second-bests are underlined.
Table 2. C-index comparison across five TCGA datasets with observed (•) and missing (◦) modalities. The bests are in bold and the second-bests are underlined.

Figure 9. Kaplan-Meier survival curves on five different datasets comparing high-risk and low-risk groups across missing pathology (P ) scenario. p-values are computed using the log-rank test. Patients are stratified into high-risk (blue) and low-risk (orange) groups based on predicted risk scores at the median threshold. Shaded regions represent 95% confidence intervals, and censored observations are indicated by vertical tick marks on the curves.

Figure 8. Kaplan-Meier survival curves on five different datasets comparing high-risk and low-risk groups across missing genomics (G) scenario. p-values are computed using the log-rank test. Patients are stratified into high-risk (blue) and low-risk (orange) groups based on predicted risk scores at the median threshold. Shaded regions represent 95% confidence intervals, and censored observations are indicated by vertical tick marks on the curves.

Figure 7. Kaplan-Meier survival curves on five different datasets comparing high-risk and low-risk groups across complete scenario. p-values are computed using the log-rank test. Patients are stratified into high-risk (blue) and low-risk (orange) groups based on predicted risk scores at the median threshold. Shaded regions represent 95% confidence intervals, and censored observations are indicated by vertical tick marks on the curves.

Figure 1. Overall architecture of MUST. The framework extracts global representations gP and gG via self-attention, computes shared information through bidirectional cross-attention, and decomposes each modality into specific and shared components for survival prediction.
Figure 4. Ablation on rank r of the shared subspace P∩.
